original_abstract: While artificial intelligence has deep historical roots, it has recently captured global attention at an unprecedented scale. Driven by innovations from tech giants like Google, Microsoft, and Meta, the landscape shifted dramatically with OpenAI’s release of ChatGPT. As a Large Language Model (LLM) built on the Transformer architecture, ChatGPT utilizes deep learning to generate sophisticated, human-like responses within conversational contexts. Its massive parameter count and open-domain training have established it as a versatile tool for diverse sectors, including automated customer service, language translation, and creative content generation. Despite its potential to revolutionize human-technology interaction, ChatGPT’s rapid adoption poses significant ethical, social, and economic risks—ranging from data privacy and algorithmic bias to workforce displacement. This article provides a comprehensive overview of ChatGPT, detailing its underlying architecture and multi-stage training processes, such as Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF). By analyzing its societal footprint, we propose a multi-faceted framework involving regulatory, educational, and technical interventions designed to maximize the model's benefits while mitigating its negative consequences. This study aims to deepen the academic understanding of LLMs and provide a foundation for predicting future shifts in the AI landscape.
humanized_abstract: As all, artificial intelligence has a rich history behind it. Now a days, it has grabbed everyone’s attention like anything. Google, Microsoft, and Meta are innovating in the field of artificial intelligence like anything, and after OpenAI brought ChatGPT, there has been a huge buzz around it in the Tech industry and even outside of it. ChatGPT is an LLM or a Large Language Model which is based on the transformer architecture using deep learning technology. It is a powerful AI model using the basic technology of deep learning having a huge parameter count after open domain training. Many organisations can already utilise the abilities of ChatGPT such as customer service bot, language translator and text generator. Along with amazing capabilities ChatGPT has also created a huge disruption and also has many drawbacks, and I mean ethically, socially and economically. These are some very critical points and we as human beings are unaware of what exactly the AI models are asking from us at times. In this post I will briefly describe the AI model ChatGPT, architecture and its multiple stages of training and models such as the Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback or RLHF. I shall then try to describe as to how is this chatGPT impacting our lives socially as well as financially and lastly I will provide with several recommendations, with regards to policy-making, on the education regarding this model and also technology-based recommendations on how can all these factors combined will be effective in maximising the benefits of this innovative work of human mind as well as in minimising the negatives. This blog will try to provide insights and also expand our academic knowledge as well as helps us in predicting the many AI developments that are set to take place in coming times.
